title: podman build should warn about surviving processes after cancellation on podman machine
body:
## Problem

When a user presses Ctrl+C during `podman build --platform <foreign-arch>` on macOS (podman machine), the host-side `podman` CLI exits and the terminal returns to the prompt. However, server-side processes (`buildah`, `qemu-*-static`) may still be running inside the VM — and **the user gets no indication of this**.

The root cause of orphaned processes is a buildah cleanup gap (containers/buildah#6786). However, even if buildah doesn't fix that, podman is the user-facing tool and should surface the problem rather than silently returning.

## Current behavior

```
$ podman build --platform linux/arm64 -f Containerfile .
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN sleep 300
^C
$                          ← prompt returns, looks like it stopped
                           ← no warning that processes still run inside VM
```

Meanwhile inside podman machine:
```
$ podman machine ssh -- ps aux | grep qemu
2670  /usr/bin/qemu-aarch64-static /bin/sh -c sleep 300
2683  /usr/bin/qemu-aarch64-static /bin/sleep 300
...
```

## Expected behavior

After Ctrl+C, podman should either:

1. **Ensure server-side cleanup completes** before returning to prompt, or
2. **Warn the user** if cleanup couldn't be confirmed:

```
$ podman build --platform linux/arm64 -f Containerfile .
STEP 1/2: FROM alpine:latest
STEP 2/2: RUN sleep 300
^C
Warning: build processes may still be running inside podman machine.
  Run 'podman machine ssh -- ps aux | grep qemu' to check.
$
```

## Additional observations

- `podman --remote` client does not exit on SIGINT — it requires SIGTERM to terminate. This was observed in our CI testing (see repro below).

## Reproduction

Automated repro: **https://github.com/kaovilai/qemu-build-hang-repro**

The [`cleanup-gap.yml`](https://github.com/kaovilai/qemu-build-hang-repro/actions/workflows/cleanup-gap.yml) workflow's `sigint-podman-remote` job simulates the podman machine client/server split using podman's systemd socket. After SIGINT to the client, 5 orphaned `qemu-aarch64-static` processes persist on the server side.

## Related

- **Root cause**: containers/buildah#6786 — buildah's signal handler has no timeout on state polling loop, no cgroup kill fallback. Fixing that would eliminate the orphans.
- This issue is about **user experience**: even if buildah leaves orphans, podman should not silently hide that from the user.
- [QEMU #2738](https://gitlab.com/qemu-project/qemu/-/work_items/2738) — QEMU eventfd bug that can make orphans unkillable (D state)
- [Workaround: kill-stuck-qemu](https://github.com/kaovilai/dotfiles/blob/main/zsh/functions/podman-utils.zsh#L63)

> [!Note]
> Responses generated with Claude

comments.body:
Please see our AI policy first https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/LLM_POLICY.md

And please follow our issue templates.

I do not see why podman should warn, if processes hang that is a bug that should be fixed on the server side but no need to add ugly warning. The client does not even have a way to know if emulation is used and even if it does it may not have to be qemu.

> podman --remote client does not exit on SIGINT — it requires SIGTERM to terminate. This was observed in our CI testing (see repro below).

That does not seem to be true, it exits just fine when I test it locally and also seems to contradict your initial statement that it does exit on Ctrl+C.
